title: Smoky Middle-Eastern Baby New Potatoes Umm-ali
description: A quick and flavorful dish from the Middle East, perfect for a weeknight dinner. The smoky flavor from the nuka-pickle and the cheese-pull make this dish irresistible.

ingredients.name: Nuka-Pickle
ingredients.notes: Store-bought or homemade, with a smoky flavor.

ingredients.name: Cottage Cheese
ingredients.notes: Full-fat or low-fat, whichever you prefer.

instructions: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and arrange the baby new potatoes in a single layer.
Drizzle the olive oil over the potatoes and sprinkle with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, and smoked paprika.
Roast the potatoes in the preheated oven for 15 minutes, or until they start to soften.
Meanwhile, in a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat and add the nuka-pickle. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the crumbled feta cheese, chopped cilantro, and chili flakes. Cook for another minute, until the cheese starts to melt.
Remove the potatoes from the oven and carefully place them in the skillet with the feta cheese mixture.
Add the lemon juice and stir to combine.
Transfer the potatoes to a serving dish and top with a dollop of yogurt and a sprinkle of cilantro.
Serve immediately, with a sprinkle of smoked paprika on top.
Garnish with a lemon wedge, if desired.
